solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    string maximumBinaryString(string binary) {
        int n = binary.length();
        int left = 0;
        int zeros = 0;

        while (left < n && binary[left] == '1') {
            left++;
        }

        for (int i = left; i < n; ++i) {
            if (binary[i] == '0') {
                ++zeros;
            }
        }

        if (zeros <= 1) {
            return binary;
        }

        int firstZero = left;
        int onesBefore = left;

        for (int i = firstZero; i < n - 1; ++i) {
            if (binary[i] == '0' && binary[i + 1] == '0') {
                binary[i] = '1';
                int j = i + 1;
                while (j < n && binary[j] == '1') {
                    ++j;
                }
                if (j < n) {
                    binary[j] = '0';
                }
            }
        }

        return binary;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution leverages the allowed operations to transform the binary string into the maximum possible value. It identifies the first group of zeros and applies transformations to shift zeros to the right, maximizing the binary value. The algorithm is efficient as it processes the string in a single pass.
